---
title: "Matthew Zhou"
subtitle: "Digital builder · Code × Analytics"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: profile.jpg
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      # Replace PROFILENAME with your LinkedIn vanity URL
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/PROFILENAME
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/mattzhou98
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:mattzhou@umich.edu"
---

I build for the web and measure what I build. My core strengths sit at the
intersection of **coding** — shipping clean front-end and tooling — and
**analysis & AI** — turning traffic and behavior data into decisions.

Right now I'm a participant in the **2026 Digital Summer Clinic**, a program run
by the Center for Digital Engagement and Ann Arbor SPARK that places interns with
high-tech startups to drive their digital marketing. As part of the clinic I'm
working a hands-on project with a SPARK incubator company, studying for the
**Google Analytics certification**, and writing here every week about what I learn.

This blog is itself a small proof of the work: it's written in
[Quarto](https://quarto.org), version-controlled on GitHub, deployed
automatically to GitHub Pages on every push, and instrumented with Google
Analytics 4 so I can see what resonates.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## A note to recruiters
I'm looking for roles where I can build digital products and use data to make them
better. If that's you, the fastest way to reach me is
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/PROFILENAME) or
[email](mailto:mattzhou@umich.edu).
:::
